scope: First DeepSeek V4-Flash-shaped one-MI350 single-layer baseline after retargeting from the prior Pro-sized harness defaults.

moe_expert_gemms_flash_target.target_scope: Whole MoE layer routed expert GEMM family in one batched/grouped call across all experts, forward plus backward. This is not a per-expert latency target.

dense_one_expert_m12288_probe_reference_.read: This direct dense one-expert M=12288 probe is retained only as a library-GEMM sanity check. It must not be compared to the 12.9 ms whole-MoE target.

moe_expert_gemms_flash_target.read: The active MoE anchor is the aggregate routed expert GEMM family for the whole MoE layer, not 1.3 ms per expert. Interpret MoE progress from routed-expert forward plus routed dgrad/wgrad subblock profiles and grouped/ragged GEMM probes.

decision.read: Retargeting to the HF Flash config changed the baseline materially: S4096 full layer is 31.70 ms, not the older Pro-sized 76 ms class result. The remaining gap to 24.9 ms is about 6.8 ms.
next_kernel_priorities: Fuse or replace sparse MLA backward softmax-score, compressed-gradient, dQ/dKV, and sink-gradient work; current Flash sparse MLA backward is about 5 ms standalone and about 9.9 ms across dgrad/sink blocks in segmented attribution.

Keep routed MoE optimization focused on the whole-MoE grouped expert GEMM family. The active anchor is about 12.9 ms fwd+bwd for all experts together; direct one-expert M=12288 GEMM timings are reference-only and should not be used as pass/fail evidence.
Run rocprofv3 selected-region capture on the Flash-shaped layer next to confirm the timer attribution against real kernel time and launch counts.
not_next: Using prior Pro-sized S4096 artifacts as pass/fail evidence for the Flash goal
Optimizing one large expert GEMM in isolation before addressing grouped/ragged routing
Treating the 8x DeepSeek-V3 16B canary throughput as proof of final V4-Flash training-system completion
